summary: The session focused on the significant growth of high-rise residential buildings in various Chinese cities, particularly Shenzhen and Guangzhou, and the challenges associated with such developments. The discussion also touched on similar global trends and the potential limits to building heights due to engineering and safety considerations.
facts: Shenzhen completed 162 high-rise residential buildings between 2015 and 2020. Guangzhou completed 145 high-rise residential buildings between 2015 and 2020. Chongqing completed 98 high-rise residential buildings between 2015 and 2020. Wuhan completed 90 high-rise residential buildings between 2015 and 2020. Shanghai completed 59 high-rise residential buildings between 2015 and 2020. The construction of high-rise buildings in cities like Shenzhen and Guangzhou has led to challenges such as overcrowding, environmental concerns, safety issues, and social displacement. Cities worldwide, including Dubai, New York, and Tokyo, face similar challenges related to high-rise building construction. Strategies to address high-rise building challenges include incorporating green spaces, using sustainable materials, improving transportation infrastructure, and engaging local communities. Factors that may restrict the height of buildings include limitations of building materials, engineering constraints, and safety considerations. Some skyscrapers are already over 800 meters tall, with some nearing or exceeding a kilometer in height.
